key_insight: EchoRisk introduces the first curated multicentre longitudinal echocardiography benchmark with explicit cardiotoxicity labels to support automated cardio-oncology risk stratification.
clinical_relevance: The dataset and tasks are designed to help identify therapy-related heart dysfunction earlier in breast cancer care, potentially supporting more consistent monitoring during treatment.
method: 422 patients from five European sites in the CARDIOCARE prospective study
2,159 echocardiography videos across 1,123 exams with up to five longitudinal timepoints
Three tasks: LVEF estimation, LV dysfunction classification, and baseline-only early cardiotoxicity prediction
evidence_signal: The abstract reports baseline R(2+1)D plus LSTM models performing strongly on functional assessment and LV dysfunction classification, while early prediction from pre-therapy imaging alone remains difficult.
watch_item: Baseline-only cardiotoxicity prediction is still an open challenge, so strong external validation will be important before clinical translation.
paper_title: EchoRisk: A Multicentre Echocardiography Dataset and Benchmark for Cardio-Oncology
